vocabulary.meaning: on / at (surface or event, + prepositional) / to (+ accusative)
vocabulary.exampleMeaning: The book is on the table.

hook.text: Prepositional is the only case that never appears without a preposition — the name says it all. Its two main jobs: location (в/на + where you are) and topic (о + what you're talking about). The endings are consistent and the context is clear. Prepositional is one of the easier cases to use correctly once you know the endings.

why.text: The confusion with в and на is that the same prepositions take accusative for direction and prepositional for location. в школу = going to school (accusative, movement); в школе = at school (prepositional, location). This в/на alternation is tested constantly because it's used in almost every sentence describing where you are or where you're going.

cards.title: Prepositional singular endings
cards.body: Prepositional singular has very regular endings. Masculine and neuter: add -Е (replace О/Е). Feminine А/Я: А→Е, Я→Е. Feminine Ь: Ь→И.

cards.title: в vs на — which to use for location
cards.body: в (in) is used for enclosed spaces and countries. на (on) is used for surfaces, open areas, events, and some fixed expressions. The choice depends on convention — some words always take на (урок, работа, улица, почта). When in doubt: в for buildings/countries, на for surfaces/events.

examples.cyrillic: в школе / в магазине / в России
examples.transliteration: v shkole / v magazine / v Rossii
examples.meaning: in school / in the shop / in Russia (enclosed, country)

examples.cyrillic: на столе / на улице / на уроке
examples.transliteration: na stole / na ulitse / na uroke
examples.meaning: on the table / outside / in the lesson (surface, open, fixed)
at work / at the post office (fixed expressions)

cards.title: в/на + location vs в/на + direction
cards.body: The same prepositions в and на take different cases for location vs direction. Location = prepositional. Direction = accusative. This is one of the most drilled pairs in Russian.

examples.meaning: The book is on the table.
Put the book on the table.

cards.title: о + prepositional — about
cards.body: о (about / concerning) always takes the prepositional case. It introduces the topic of speech, thought, or concern.

examples.meaning: He is talking about the book.

questions.explanation: школа → школе. Feminine А→Е in prepositional.

questions.prompt: I am in school: Я ___ школе.
questions.explanation: в + prepositional = location in an enclosed space. Я в школе.

questions.prompt: The book is on the table: Книга ___ столе.
questions.explanation: на + prepositional = on a surface. Книга на столе.

cards.title: ⚠ Location vs direction — the most tested pair
cards.body: The в/на location (prepositional) vs в/на direction (accusative) distinction is one of the most common error types in Russian. The test: is there movement toward? → accusative. Is someone/something already there? → prepositional. Practise both sides of each pair together.

examples.cyrillic: Я в офисе. vs Я иду в офис.
examples.transliteration: Ya v ofise / Ya idu v ofis.
examples.meaning: I am in the office (prep.) / I'm going to the office (acc.)

examples.cyrillic: Он на работе. vs Он идёт на работу.
examples.transliteration: On na rabote / On idyot na rabotu.
examples.meaning: He is at work (prep.) / He is going to work (acc.)

cards.title: Fixed expressions with на
cards.body: Some location words always use на rather than в — this must be memorised as vocabulary. The most important: работа (work), улица (street), урок (lesson), почта (post office), рынок (market), концерт (concert), собрание (meeting).

questions.prompt: I am at work: Я ___ работе.
questions.explanation: работа is one of the fixed на-words. Я на работе.

questions.prompt: He is in Russia: Он ___ России.
questions.explanation: Countries always use в for location. Он в России.

questions.prompt: I am thinking about work: Я думаю ___ работе.
questions.explanation: о + prepositional = about. Я думаю о работе.

questions.prompt: He is going to work (direction): Он идёт ___ работу.
questions.explanation: Direction uses accusative. работа → работу. на работу = going to work.

questions.prompt: We are in the lesson: Мы ___ уроке.
questions.explanation: урок is a fixed на-word. Мы на уроке.

rules: Prepositional singular: masculine/neuter +Е, feminine А→Е, feminine Ь→И
в + prepositional = location in enclosed space/country; на + prepositional = on surface or fixed expressions
⚠ в/на + accusative = direction; в/на + prepositional = location
Fixed на-words: работа, улица, урок, почта, рынок, концерт
summary.nextLesson: Next: Case review — all six cases compared in parallel, with mixed drills across the full paradigm.

questions.prompt: I am in school: Я в ___.
I am going to school: Я иду в ___.
He is at work uses which preposition?
I am thinking about the book: Я думаю о ___. (книга)
Location uses which case with в/на?
